explicit_text: I was born and raised in Tacoma, Washington. My family lived there before the war, and it was near Puget Sound where my father and grandfather went fishing. Tacoma was also where I attended school before evacuation, and the community had some support for us, like the mayor who opposed the evacuation. Growing up there during the Depression was tough, but it was home.
implicit_text: I was born and raised in a city in Washington state near Puget Sound. My family lived there before the war, and it was close to where my father and grandfather went fishing. That city was also where I attended school before evacuation, and the community had some support for us, like the mayor who opposed the evacuation. Growing up there during the Depression was tough, but it was home.

explicit_text: Our first stop after evacuation was the Pinedale Assembly Center in Fresno County, California. We stayed there for about three months before being sent to Tule Lake. It was a temporary place where many families like mine were gathered before being moved to the more permanent camps.
implicit_text: Our first stop after evacuation was an assembly center located in Fresno County, California. We stayed there for about three months before being sent to a more permanent camp in northern California. It was a temporary place where many families like mine were gathered before being moved to the more permanent camps.

explicit_text: Pinedale Assembly Center, where we first went after evacuation, was located in Fresno County, California. It was a brief stop for us before being sent to Tule Lake. Fresno County was part of the route many Japanese American families took during the forced relocation.
implicit_text: The assembly center where we first went after evacuation was located in Fresno County, California. It was a brief stop for us before being sent to a camp in northern California. Fresno County was part of the route many Japanese American families took during the forced relocation.

explicit_text: After Pinedale, we were sent to the Tule Lake War Relocation Center, which was a huge, dusty camp. I spent about a year there during my teenage years. It was tough but I managed to find some normalcy with friends and even got a waitress job using a relative's name to earn spending money. Tule Lake was also where my father made the decision to pledge loyalty to the U.S. rather than return to Japan.
implicit_text: After the assembly center, we were sent to a huge, dusty camp in northern California. I spent about a year there during my teenage years. It was tough but I managed to find some normalcy with friends and even got a waitress job using a relative's name to earn spending money. That camp was also where my father made the decision to pledge loyalty to the U.S. rather than return to Japan.

explicit_text: Many people from Washington wanted to go to Minidoka, but because my family signed up as a group, we were sent elsewhere. I never lived at Minidoka, but it was a preferred camp for many from my home state. Our group ended up going to Jerome instead.
implicit_text: Many people from Washington wanted to go to a camp in Idaho, but because my family signed up as a group, we were sent elsewhere. I never lived at that camp, but it was a preferred location for many from my home state. Our group ended up going to a camp in Arkansas instead.

explicit_text: My family was sent to the Jerome War Relocation Center in Arkansas after Tule Lake. Jerome was a swampy place, and I spent my junior year of high school there. The camp closed after about a year, so we were moved again. It was a difficult environment but part of my wartime experience.
implicit_text: My family was sent to a relocation center in Arkansas after the camp in northern California. That place was swampy, and I spent my junior year of high school there. The camp closed after about a year, so we were moved again. It was a difficult environment but part of my wartime experience.

explicit_text: After Jerome closed, we were sent to Heart Mountain in Wyoming, near Yellowstone National Park. I spent my senior year of high school there and even got to visit Yellowstone on a $5 chartered bus trip. Life at Heart Mountain became more relaxed toward the end, and I had a better social life, including going to dances with my father's permission.
implicit_text: After the Arkansas camp closed, we were sent to a relocation center in Wyoming, near a famous national park. I spent my senior year of high school there and even got to visit that national park on a $5 chartered bus trip. Life at this Wyoming camp became more relaxed toward the end, and I had a better social life, including going to dances with my father's permission.

explicit_text: While at Heart Mountain, I had the chance to visit Yellowstone National Park. For just five dollars, they chartered a bus and took us there. It was a rare and special outing during the confinement of camp life, giving me a brief escape to nature.
implicit_text: While at the Wyoming camp, I had the chance to visit a famous national park. For just five dollars, they chartered a bus and took us there. It was a rare and special outing during the confinement of camp life, giving me a brief escape to nature.

explicit_text: Near Heart Mountain, we would sometimes walk out of camp and go to the Shoshone River for weenie bakes. It was a way to enjoy some freedom and socialize outside the camp gates. We were even invited by local Boys Clubs for such events, which made that time somewhat enjoyable despite the circumstances.
implicit_text: Near the Wyoming camp, we would sometimes walk out of camp and go to a nearby river for weenie bakes. It was a way to enjoy some freedom and socialize outside the camp gates. We were even invited by local Boys Clubs for such events, which made that time somewhat enjoyable despite the circumstances.

explicit_text: Growing up during the Great Depression was really hard for my family. My father had a produce market but had to file for bankruptcy, and he took on menial jobs like shucking oysters. We lived in poverty, with an icebox instead of a refrigerator, and wore very cheap clothes. It was a tough time, but we managed to get by and eventually started a laundry business before the war.
implicit_text: Growing up during the economic downturn of the 1930s was really hard for my family. My father had a produce market but had to file for bankruptcy, and he took on menial jobs like shucking oysters. We lived in poverty, with an icebox instead of a refrigerator, and wore very cheap clothes. It was a tough time, but we managed to get by and eventually started a laundry business before the war.

explicit_text: I remember the day Pearl Harbor was attacked; it was a Sunday. I was coming home from Buddhist Church Sunday school when I heard men talking about it. My mother found out quickly and was worried because my father and grandfather were out fishing near Puget Sound. That same day, the FBI came for my grandfather, who was a community leader, and took him away.
implicit_text: I remember the day the naval base in Hawaii was attacked; it was a Sunday. I was coming home from Buddhist Church Sunday school when I heard men talking about it. My mother found out quickly and was worried because my father and grandfather were out fishing near Puget Sound. That same day, federal agents came for my grandfather, who was a community leader, and took him away.

explicit_text: The FBI came to our home the day Pearl Harbor was attacked and took my grandfather away because he was a civic leader involved in church and the Japanese language school. We didn't know where they took him at first, but later found out he was sent to camps in Missoula, Montana, and then to Santa Fe and Lordsburg, New Mexico. It was a frightening and confusing time for our family.
implicit_text: Federal agents came to our home the day the naval base in Hawaii was attacked and took my grandfather away because he was a civic leader involved in church and the Japanese language school. We didn't know where they took him at first, but later found out he was sent to detention camps in Montana, and then to camps in New Mexico. It was a frightening and confusing time for our family.

explicit_text: My grandfather was taken by the FBI to a camp in Missoula, Montana after Pearl Harbor. I heard from an Italian man who was there that they had a mock hearing. It was a harsh experience, and my grandfather was later transferred to other camps in New Mexico before being released after about a year.
implicit_text: My grandfather was taken by federal agents to a camp in Montana after the attack on the naval base. I heard from an Italian man who was there that they had a mock hearing. It was a harsh experience, and my grandfather was later transferred to other camps in New Mexico before being released after about a year.

explicit_text: After Missoula, my grandfather was sent to a camp in Santa Fe, New Mexico. This was part of his journey through the FBI detention camps, which were very different and harsher than the camps where my family was held. I kept writing to him weekly in my broken Japanese during this time.
implicit_text: After Montana, my grandfather was sent to a camp in New Mexico. This was part of his journey through federal detention camps, which were very different and harsher than the camps where my family was held. I kept writing to him weekly in my broken Japanese during this time.

explicit_text: My grandfather was also held at Lordsburg, New Mexico, after being in Missoula and Santa Fe. These FBI camps were very tough, and I wish I had been older to ask him about his experiences. He came back to Tule Lake after his release, but he had aged noticeably from the ordeal.
implicit_text: My grandfather was also held at another camp in New Mexico after being in Montana and Santa Fe. These federal camps were very tough, and I wish I had been older to ask him about his experiences. He came back to the northern California camp after his release, but he had aged noticeably from the ordeal.

explicit_text: My mother was born in Napa, California in 1907. When she was about seven, her family took her to Japan where she lived for ten years with her grandparents on her father's side. Napa was her birthplace, but she spent much of her childhood separated from her parents due to that trip.
implicit_text: My mother was born in a city in northern California in 1907. When she was about seven, her family took her to Asia where she lived for ten years with her grandparents on her father's side. That city was her birthplace, but she spent much of her childhood separated from her parents due to that trip.

explicit_text: Japan was a place my mother lived for ten years as a child, raised by her grandparents while her parents stayed in the U.S. My grandfather's family came from samurai stock, and I have documents tracing our family history there over 600 years. My father decided not to return to Japan after the loyalty oath, wanting to raise his children in America.
implicit_text: The Asian country was a place my mother lived for ten years as a child, raised by her grandparents while her parents stayed in the U.S. My grandfather's family came from samurai stock, and I have documents tracing our family history there over 600 years. My father decided not to return there after the loyalty oath, wanting to raise his children in America.

explicit_text: We lived near Puget Sound in Washington, and both my father and grandfather would go fishing there. On the day of Pearl Harbor, they were out fishing, which caused worry at home. Puget Sound was part of our daily life before evacuation.
implicit_text: We lived near a large sound in Washington, and both my father and grandfather would go fishing there. On the day of the naval base attack, they were out fishing, which caused worry at home. That body of water was part of our daily life before evacuation.

explicit_text: While at Tule Lake, I wanted to buy things from the Montgomery Ward catalog, which was a big deal for me. I worked as a waitress using a relative's name to get paid, and that money helped me buy personal items. It was a way to have some independence and normal teenage desires even in camp.
implicit_text: While at the northern California camp, I wanted to buy things from a popular mail-order catalog, which was a big deal for me. I worked as a waitress using a relative's name to get paid, and that money helped me buy personal items. It was a way to have some independence and normal teenage desires even in camp.

explicit_text: Fresh tuna was brought into the camps from places like Denver, which my mother would sometimes bring home from the mess hall. It was part of the food supply in camp, and my mother occasionally worked in the canteen. Denver was a source of some of the food we ate during our confinement.
implicit_text: Fresh tuna was brought into the camps from places like a major city in Colorado, which my mother would sometimes bring home from the mess hall. It was part of the food supply in camp, and my mother occasionally worked in the canteen. That city was a source of some of the food we ate during our confinement.

explicit_text: My boyfriend during camp left for Nebraska because his girlfriend was there. We met in camp, but after she returned from Nebraska, he married her. Nebraska was part of the story of relationships and separations during the war years.
implicit_text: My boyfriend during camp left for a Midwestern state because his girlfriend was there. We met in camp, but after she returned from that state, he married her. That state was part of the story of relationships and separations during the war years.

explicit_text: After the war, my husband and I traveled extensively, including going through the Panama Canal. We also visited Japan and Europe multiple times. Traveling was a big part of our life together and something I continue to enjoy with my children now.
implicit_text: After the war, my husband and I traveled extensively, including going through a famous canal in Central America. We also visited the Asian country and Europe multiple times. Traveling was a big part of our life together and something I continue to enjoy with my children now.
